22. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": "test/integration/targets/module_utils_selinux/tasks/selinux.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/ansible--4c5ce5a1"
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: - name: collect selinux facts\\n2:   setup:\\n3:     gather_subset: ['!all', '!min', selinux]\\n4:   register: fact_output\\n5: \\n6: - debug:\\n7:     var: fact_output\\n8: \\n9: - name: create tempdir container in home\\n10:   file:\\n11:     path: ~/.selinux_tmp\\n12:     state: directory\\n13: \\n14: - name: create tempdir\\n15:   tempfile:\\n16:     path: ~/.selinux_tmp\\n17:     prefix: selinux_test\\n18:     state: directory\\n19:   register: tempdir\\n20: \\n21: - name: ls -1Zd tempdir to capture context from FS\\n22:   shell: ls -1Zd '{{ tempdir.path }}'\\n23:   register: tempdir_context_output\\n24: \\n25: - name: create a file under the tempdir with no context info specified (it should inherit parent context)\\n26:   file:\\n27:     path: '{{ tempdir.path }}/file_inherited_context'\\n28:     state: touch\\n29:   register: file_inherited_context\\n30: \\n31: - name: ls -1Z inherited file to capture context from FS\\n32:   shell: ls -1Z '{{ tempdir.path }}/file_inherited_context'\\n33:   register: inherited_context_output\\n34: \\n35: - name: copy the file with explicit overrides on all context values\\n36:   copy:\\n37:     remote_src: yes\\n38:     src: '{{ tempdir.path }}/file_inherited_context'\\n39:     dest: '{{ tempdir.path }}/file_explicit_context'\\n40:     seuser: system_u\\n41:     serole: system_r\\n42:     setype: user_tmp_t\\n43:     # default configs don't have MLS levels defined, so we can't test that yet\\n44:     # selevel: s1\\n45:   register: file_explicit_context\\n46: \\n47: - name: ls -1Z explicit file to capture context from FS\\n48:   shell: ls -1Z '{{ tempdir.path }}/file_explicit_context'\\n49:   register: explicit_context_output\\n50: \\n51: - name: alter the tempdir context\\n52:   file:\\n53:     path: '{{ tempdir.path }}'\\n54:     seuser: system_u\\n55:     serole: system_r\\n56:     setype: user_tmp_t\\n57:     # default configs don't have MLS levels defined, so we can't test that yet\\n58:     # selevel: s1\\n59:   register: tempdir_altered\\n60: \\n61: - name: ls -1Z tempdir to capture context from FS\\n62:   shell: ls -1Z '{{ tempdir.path }}/file_explicit_context'\\n63:   register: tempdir_altered_context_output\\n64: \\n65: - name: copy the explicit context file with default overrides on all context values\\n66:   copy:\\n67:     remote_src: yes\\n68:     src: '{{ tempdir.path }}/file_explicit_context'\\n69:     dest: '{{ tempdir.path }}/file_default_context'\\n70:     seuser: _default\\n71:     serole: _default\\n72:     setype: _default\\n73:     selevel: _default\\n74:   register: file_default_context\\n75: \\n76: - name: see what matchpathcon thinks the context of default_file_context should be\\n77:   shell: matchpathcon {{ file_default_context.dest }} | awk '{ print $2 }'\\n78:   register: expected_default_context\\n79: \\n80: - assert:\\n81:     that:\\n82:     - fact_output.ansible_facts.ansible_selinux.config_mode in ['enforcing','permissive']\\n83:     - fact_output.ansible_facts.ansible_selinux.mode in ['enforcing','permissive']\\n84:     - fact_output.ansible_facts.ansible_selinux.status == 'enabled'\\n85:     - fact_output.ansible_facts.ansible_selinux_python_present == true\\n86:     # assert that secontext is set on the file results (injected by basic.py, for better or worse)\\n87:     - tempdir.secontext is match('.+:.+:.+') and tempdir.secontext in tempdir_context_output.stdout\\n88:     - file_inherited_context.secontext is match('.+:.+:.+') and file_inherited_context.secontext in inherited_context_output.stdout\\n89:     - file_inherited_context.secontext == tempdir.secontext  # should've been inherited from the parent dir since not set explicitly\\n90:     - file_explicit_context.secontext == 'system_u:system_r:user_tmp_t:s0' and file_explicit_context.secontext in explicit_context_output.stdout\\n91:     - tempdir_altered.secontext == 'system_u:system_r:user_tmp_t:s0' and tempdir_altered.secontext in tempdir_altered_context_output.stdout\\n92:     # the one with reset defaults should match the original tempdir context, not the altered one (ie, it was set by the original policy context, not inherited from the parent dir)\\n93:     - file_default_context.secontext == expected_default_context.stdout_lines[0]\\n94: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/ansible--4c5ce5a1@4c5ce5a1a9e79a845aff4978cfeb72a0d4ecf7d6/-/blob/test/integration/targets/module_utils_selinux/tasks/selinux.yml\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: - name: collect selinux facts\n2:   setup:\n3:     gather_subset: ['!all', '!min', selinux]\n4:   register: fact_output\n5: \n6: - debug:\n7:     var: fact_output\n8: \n9: - name: create tempdir container in home\n10:   file:\n11:     path: ~/.selinux_tmp\n12:     state: directory\n13: \n14: - name: create tempdir\n15:   tempfile:\n16:     path: ~/.selinux_tmp\n17:     prefix: selinux_test\n18:     state: directory\n19:   register: tempdir\n20: \n21: - name: ls -1Zd tempdir to capture context from FS\n22:   shell: ls -1Zd '{{ tempdir.path }}'\n23:   register: tempdir_context_output\n24: \n25: - name: create a file under the tempdir with no context info specified (it should inherit parent context)\n26:   file:\n27:     path: '{{ tempdir.path }}/file_inherited_context'\n28:     state: touch\n29:   register: file_inherited_context\n30: \n31: - name: ls -1Z inherited file to capture context from FS\n32:   shell: ls -1Z '{{ tempdir.path }}/file_inherited_context'\n33:   register: inherited_context_output\n34: \n35: - name: copy the file with explicit overrides on all context values\n36:   copy:\n37:     remote_src: yes\n38:     src: '{{ tempdir.path }}/file_inherited_context'\n39:     dest: '{{ tempdir.path }}/file_explicit_context'\n40:     seuser: system_u\n41:     serole: system_r\n42:     setype: user_tmp_t\n43:     # default configs don't have MLS levels defined, so we can't test that yet\n44:     # selevel: s1\n45:   register: file_explicit_context\n46: \n47: - name: ls -1Z explicit file to capture context from FS\n48:   shell: ls -1Z '{{ tempdir.path }}/file_explicit_context'\n49:   register: explicit_context_output\n50: \n51: - name: alter the tempdir context\n52:   file:\n53:     path: '{{ tempdir.path }}'\n54:     seuser: system_u\n55:     serole: system_r\n56:     setype: user_tmp_t\n57:     # default configs don't have MLS levels defined, so we can't test that yet\n58:     # selevel: s1\n59:   register: tempdir_altered\n60: \n61: - name: ls -1Z tempdir to capture context from FS\n62:   shell: ls -1Z '{{ tempdir.path }}/file_explicit_context'\n63:   register: tempdir_altered_context_output\n64: \n65: - name: copy the explicit context file with default overrides on all context values\n66:   copy:\n67:     remote_src: yes\n68:     src: '{{ tempdir.path }}/file_explicit_context'\n69:     dest: '{{ tempdir.path }}/file_default_context'\n70:     seuser: _default\n71:     serole: _default\n72:     setype: _default\n73:     selevel: _default\n74:   register: file_default_context\n75: \n76: - name: see what matchpathcon thinks the context of default_file_context should be\n77:   shell: matchpathcon {{ file_default_context.dest }} | awk '{ print $2 }'\n78:   register: expected_default_context\n79: \n80: - assert:\n81:     that:\n82:     - fact_output.ansible_facts.ansible_selinux.config_mode in ['enforcing','permissive']\n83:     - fact_output.ansible_facts.ansible_selinux.mode in ['enforcing','permissive']\n84:     - fact_output.ansible_facts.ansible_selinux.status == 'enabled'\n85:     - fact_output.ansible_facts.ansible_selinux_python_present == true\n86:     # assert that secontext is set on the file results (injected by basic.py, for better or worse)\n87:     - tempdir.secontext is match('.+:.+:.+') and tempdir.secontext in tempdir_context_output.stdout\n88:     - file_inherited_context.secontext is match('.+:.+:.+') and file_inherited_context.secontext in inherited_context_output.stdout\n89:     - file_inherited_context.secontext == tempdir.secontext  # should've been inherited from the parent dir since not set explicitly\n90:     - file_explicit_context.secontext == 'system_u:system_r:user_tmp_t:s0' and file_explicit_context.secontext in explicit_context_output.stdout\n91:     - tempdir_altered.secontext == 'system_u:system_r:user_tmp_t:s0' and tempdir_altered.secontext in tempdir_altered_context_output.stdout\n92:     # the one with reset defaults should match the original tempdir context, not the altered one (ie, it was set by the original policy context, not inherited from the parent dir)\n93:     - file_default_context.secontext == expected_default_context.stdout_lines[0]\n94: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/ansible--4c5ce5a1@4c5ce5a1a9e79a845aff4978cfeb72a0d4ecf7d6/-/blob/test/integration/targets/module_utils_selinux/tasks/selinux.yml"
  }
}
23. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": "test/integration/targets/module_utils_respawn/library/respawnme.py",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/ansible--4c5ce5a1"
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: #!/usr/bin/python\\n2: \\n3: # Copyright: (c) 2021, Ansible Project\\n4: # GNU General Public License v3.0+ (see COPYING or https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-3.0.txt)\\n5: \\n6: from __future__ import (absolute_import, division, print_function)\\n7: __metaclass__ = type\\n8: \\n9: import os\\n10: import sys\\n11: \\n12: from ansible.module_utils.basic import AnsibleModule\\n13: from ansible.module_utils.common.respawn import respawn_module, has_respawned\\n14: \\n15: \\n16: def main():\\n17:     mod = AnsibleModule(argument_spec=dict(\\n18:         mode=dict(required=True, choices=['multi_respawn', 'no_respawn', 'respawn'])\\n19:     ))\\n20: \\n21:     # just return info about what interpreter we're currently running under\\n22:     if mod.params['mode'] == 'no_respawn':\\n23:         mod.exit_json(interpreter_path=sys.executable)\\n24:     elif mod.params['mode'] == 'respawn':\\n25:         if not has_respawned():\\n26:             new_interpreter = os.path.join(mod.tmpdir, 'anotherpython')\\n27:             os.symlink(sys.executable, new_interpreter)\\n28:             respawn_module(interpreter_path=new_interpreter)\\n29: \\n30:             # respawn should always exit internally- if we continue executing here, it's a bug\\n31:             raise Exception('FAIL, should never reach this line')\\n32:         else:\\n33:             # return the current interpreter, as well as a signal that we created a different one\\n34:             mod.exit_json(created_interpreter=sys.executable, interpreter_path=sys.executable)\\n35:     elif mod.params['mode'] == 'multi_respawn':\\n36:         # blindly respawn with the current interpreter, the second time should bomb\\n37:         respawn_module(sys.executable)\\n38: \\n39:     # shouldn't be any way for us to fall through, but just in case, that's also a bug\\n40:     raise Exception('FAIL, should never reach this code')\\n41: \\n42: \\n43: if __name__ == '__main__':\\n44:     main()\\n45: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/ansible--4c5ce5a1@4c5ce5a1a9e79a845aff4978cfeb72a0d4ecf7d6/-/blob/test/integration/targets/module_utils_respawn/library/respawnme.py\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: #!/usr/bin/python\n2: \n3: # Copyright: (c) 2021, Ansible Project\n4: # GNU General Public License v3.0+ (see COPYING or https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-3.0.txt)\n5: \n6: from __future__ import (absolute_import, division, print_function)\n7: __metaclass__ = type\n8: \n9: import os\n10: import sys\n11: \n12: from ansible.module_utils.basic import AnsibleModule\n13: from ansible.module_utils.common.respawn import respawn_module, has_respawned\n14: \n15: \n16: def main():\n17:     mod = AnsibleModule(argument_spec=dict(\n18:         mode=dict(required=True, choices=['multi_respawn', 'no_respawn', 'respawn'])\n19:     ))\n20: \n21:     # just return info about what interpreter we're currently running under\n22:     if mod.params['mode'] == 'no_respawn':\n23:         mod.exit_json(interpreter_path=sys.executable)\n24:     elif mod.params['mode'] == 'respawn':\n25:         if not has_respawned():\n26:             new_interpreter = os.path.join(mod.tmpdir, 'anotherpython')\n27:             os.symlink(sys.executable, new_interpreter)\n28:             respawn_module(interpreter_path=new_interpreter)\n29: \n30:             # respawn should always exit internally- if we continue executing here, it's a bug\n31:             raise Exception('FAIL, should never reach this line')\n32:         else:\n33:             # return the current interpreter, as well as a signal that we created a different one\n34:             mod.exit_json(created_interpreter=sys.executable, interpreter_path=sys.executable)\n35:     elif mod.params['mode'] == 'multi_respawn':\n36:         # blindly respawn with the current interpreter, the second time should bomb\n37:         respawn_module(sys.executable)\n38: \n39:     # shouldn't be any way for us to fall through, but just in case, that's also a bug\n40:     raise Exception('FAIL, should never reach this code')\n41: \n42: \n43: if __name__ == '__main__':\n44:     main()\n45: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/ansible--4c5ce5a1@4c5ce5a1a9e79a845aff4978cfeb72a0d4ecf7d6/-/blob/test/integration/targets/module_utils_respawn/library/respawnme.py"
  }
}

  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: # Copyright (c), Michael DeHaan <michael.dehaan@gmail.com>, 2012-2013\\n2: # Copyright (c), Toshio Kuratomi <tkuratomi@ansible.com> 2016\\n3: # Simplified BSD License (see licenses/simplified_bsd.txt or https://opensource.org/licenses/BSD-2-Clause)\\n4: \\n5: from __future__ import absolute_import, division, print_function\\n6: __metaclass__ = type\\n7: \\n8: FILE_ATTRIBUTES = {\\n9:     'A': 'noatime',\\n10:     'a': 'append',\\n11:     'c': 'compressed',\\n12:     'C': 'nocow',\\n13:     'd': 'nodump',\\n14:     'D': 'dirsync',\\n15:     'e': 'extents',\\n16:     'E': 'encrypted',\\n17:     'h': 'blocksize',\\n18:     'i': 'immutable',\\n19:     'I': 'indexed',\\n20:     'j': 'journalled',\\n21:     'N': 'inline',\\n22:     's': 'zero',\\n23:     'S': 'synchronous',\\n24:     't': 'notail',\\n25:     'T': 'blockroot',\\n26:     'u': 'undelete',\\n27:     'X': 'compressedraw',\\n28:     'Z': 'compresseddirty',\\n29: }\\n30: \\n31: # Ansible modules can be written in any language.\\n32: # The functions available here can be used to do many common tasks,\\n33: # to simplify development of Python modules.\\n34: \\n35: import __main__\\n36: import atexit\\n37: import errno\\n38: import datetime\\n39: import grp\\n40: import fcntl\\n41: import locale\\n42: import os\\n43: import pwd\\n44: import platform\\n45: import re\\n46: import select\\n47: import shlex\\n48: import shutil\\n49: import signal\\n50: import stat\\n51: import subprocess\\n52: import sys\\n53: import tempfile\\n54: import time\\n55: import traceback\\n56: import types\\n57: \\n58: from collections import deque\\n59: from itertools import chain, repeat\\n60: \\n61: try:\\n62:     import syslog\\n63:     HAS_SYSLOG = True\\n64: except ImportError:\\n65:     HAS_SYSLOG = False\\n66: \\n67: try:\\n68:     from systemd import journal\\n69:     # Makes sure that systemd.journal has method sendv()\\n70:     # Double check that journal has method sendv (some packages don't)\\n71:     has_journal = hasattr(journal, 'sendv')\\n72: except ImportError:\\n73:     has_journal = False\\n74: \\n75: HAVE_SELINUX = False\\n76: try:\\n77:     import ansible.module_utils.compat.selinux as selinux\\n78:     HAVE_SELINUX = True\\n79: except ImportError:\\n80:     pass\\n81: \\n82: # Python2 & 3 way to get NoneType\\n83: NoneType = type(None)\\n84: \\n85: from ansible.module_utils.compat import selectors\\n86: \\n87: from ._text import to_native, to_bytes, to_text\\n88: from ansible.module_utils.common.text.converters import (\\n89:     jsonify,\\n90:     container_to_bytes as json_dict_unicode_to_bytes,\\n91:     container_to_text as json_dict_bytes_to_unicode,\\n92: )\\n93: \\n94: from ansible.module_utils.common.text.formatters import (\\n95:     lenient_lowercase,\\n96:     bytes_to_human,\\n97:     human_to_bytes,\\n98:     SIZE_RANGES,\\n99: )\\n100: \\n101: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/ansible--4c5ce5a1@4c5ce5a1a9e79a845aff4978cfeb72a0d4ecf7d6/-/blob/lib/ansible/module_utils/basic.py%3FL1-100\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: # Copyright (c), Michael DeHaan <michael.dehaan@gmail.com>, 2012-2013\n2: # Copyright (c), Toshio Kuratomi <tkuratomi@ansible.com> 2016\n3: # Simplified BSD License (see licenses/simplified_bsd.txt or https://opensource.org/licenses/BSD-2-Clause)\n4: \n5: from __future__ import absolute_import, division, print_function\n6: __metaclass__ = type\n7: \n8: FILE_ATTRIBUTES = {\n9:     'A': 'noatime',\n10:     'a': 'append',\n11:     'c': 'compressed',\n12:     'C': 'nocow',\n13:     'd': 'nodump',\n14:     'D': 'dirsync',\n15:     'e': 'extents',\n16:     'E': 'encrypted',\n17:     'h': 'blocksize',\n18:     'i': 'immutable',\n19:     'I': 'indexed',\n20:     'j': 'journalled',\n21:     'N': 'inline',\n22:     's': 'zero',\n23:     'S': 'synchronous',\n24:     't': 'notail',\n25:     'T': 'blockroot',\n26:     'u': 'undelete',\n27:     'X': 'compressedraw',\n28:     'Z': 'compresseddirty',\n29: }\n30: \n31: # Ansible modules can be written in any language.\n32: # The functions available here can be used to do many common tasks,\n33: # to simplify development of Python modules.\n34: \n35: import __main__\n36: import atexit\n37: import errno\n38: import datetime\n39: import grp\n40: import fcntl\n41: import locale\n42: import os\n43: import pwd\n44: import platform\n45: import re\n46: import select\n47: import shlex\n48: import shutil\n49: import signal\n50: import stat\n51: import subprocess\n52: import sys\n53: import tempfile\n54: import time\n55: import traceback\n56: import types\n57: \n58: from collections import deque\n59: from itertools import chain, repeat\n60: \n61: try:\n62:     import syslog\n63:     HAS_SYSLOG = True\n64: except ImportError:\n65:     HAS_SYSLOG = False\n66: \n67: try:\n68:     from systemd import journal\n69:     # Makes sure that systemd.journal has method sendv()\n70:     # Double check that journal has method sendv (some packages don't)\n71:     has_journal = hasattr(journal, 'sendv')\n72: except ImportError:\n73:     has_journal = False\n74: \n75: HAVE_SELINUX = False\n76: try:\n77:     import ansible.module_utils.compat.selinux as selinux\n78:     HAVE_SELINUX = True\n79: except ImportError:\n80:     pass\n81: \n82: # Python2 & 3 way to get NoneType\n83: NoneType = type(None)\n84: \n85: from ansible.module_utils.compat import selectors\n86: \n87: from ._text import to_native, to_bytes, to_text\n88: from ansible.module_utils.common.text.converters import (\n89:     jsonify,\n90:     container_to_bytes as json_dict_unicode_to_bytes,\n91:     container_to_text as json_dict_bytes_to_unicode,\n92: )\n93: \n94: from ansible.module_utils.common.text.formatters import (\n95:     lenient_lowercase,\n96:     bytes_to_human,\n97:     human_to_bytes,\n98:     SIZE_RANGES,\n99: )\n100: \n101: ",

Output
{
  "content": "{\"blocks\":[{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"876: \\n877:     # Detect whether using selinux that is MLS-aware.\\n878:     # While this means you can set the level/range with\\n879:     # selinux.lsetfilecon(), it may or may not mean that you\\n880:     # will get the selevel as part of the context returned\\n881:     # by selinux.lgetfilecon().\\n882: \\n883:     def selinux_mls_enabled(self):\\n884:         if self._selinux_mls_enabled is None:\\n885:             self._selinux_mls_enabled = HAVE_SELINUX and selinux.is_selinux_mls_enabled() == 1\\n886: \\n887:         return self._selinux_mls_enabled\\n888: \\n889:     def selinux_enabled(self):\\n890:         if self._selinux_enabled is None:\\n891:             self._selinux_enabled = HAVE_SELINUX and selinux.is_selinux_enabled() == 1\\n892: \\n893:         return self._selinux_enabled\\n894: \\n895: \",\"endLine\":895,\"startLine\":876},{\"content\":\"74: \\n75: HAVE_SELINUX = False\\n76: try:\\n77:     import ansible.module_utils.compat.selinux as selinux\\n78:     HAVE_SELINUX = True\\n79: except ImportError:\\n80: \",\"endLine\":80,\"startLine\":74},{\"content\":\"919: \\n920:     def selinux_context(self, path):\\n921:         context = self.selinux_initial_context()\\n922:         if not self.selinux_enabled():\\n923:             return context\\n924: \",\"endLine\":924,\"startLine\":919}],\"file\":\"lib/ansible/module_utils/basic.py\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/ansible--4c5ce5a1\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/ansible--4c5ce5a1/-/blob/lib/ansible/module_utils/basic.py\"}]}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "blocks": [
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "876: \n877:     # Detect whether using selinux that is MLS-aware.\n878:     # While this means you can set the level/range with\n879:     # selinux.lsetfilecon(), it may or may not mean that you\n880:     # will get the selevel as part of the context returned\n881:     # by selinux.lgetfilecon().\n882: \n883:     def selinux_mls_enabled(self):\n884:         if self._selinux_mls_enabled is None:\n885:             self._selinux_mls_enabled = HAVE_SELINUX and selinux.is_selinux_mls_enabled() == 1\n886: \n887:         return self._selinux_mls_enabled\n888: \n889:     def selinux_enabled(self):\n890:         if self._selinux_enabled is None:\n891:             self._selinux_enabled = HAVE_SELINUX and selinux.is_selinux_enabled() == 1\n892: \n893:         return self._selinux_enabled\n894: \n895: ",
            "endLine": 895,
            "startLine": 876
          },
          {
            "content": "74: \n75: HAVE_SELINUX = False\n76: try:\n77:     import ansible.module_utils.compat.selinux as selinux\n78:     HAVE_SELINUX = True\n79: except ImportError:\n80: ",
            "endLine": 80,
            "startLine": 74
          },
          {
            "content": "919: \n920:     def selinux_context(self, path):\n921:         context = self.selinux_initial_context()\n922:         if not self.selinux_enabled():\n923:             return context\n924: ",
            "endLine": 924,
            "startLine": 919
          }
        ],

Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: #!/usr/bin/python\\n2: # -*- coding: utf-8 -*-\\n3: \\n4: # Copyright: (c) 2012, Flowroute LLC\\n5: # Written by Matthew Williams <matthew@flowroute.com>\\n6: # Based on yum module written by Seth Vidal <skvidal at fedoraproject.org>\\n7: \\n8: # GNU General Public License v3.0+ (see COPYING or https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-3.0.txt)\\n9: \\n10: from __future__ import absolute_import, division, print_function\\n11: __metaclass__ = type\\n12: \\n13: \\n14: DOCUMENTATION = '''\\n15: ---\\n16: module: apt\\n17: short_description: Manages apt-packages\\n18: description:\\n19:   - Manages I(apt) packages (such as for Debian/Ubuntu).\\n20: version_added: \\\"0.0.2\\\"\\n21: options:\\n22:   name:\\n23:     description:\\n24:       - A list of package names, like C(foo), or package specifier with version, like C(foo=1.0).\\n25:         Name wildcards (fnmatch) like C(apt*) and version wildcards like C(foo=1.0*) are also supported.\\n26:     aliases: [ package, pkg ]\\n27:     type: list\\n28:     elements: str\\n29:   state:\\n30:     description:\\n31:       - Indicates the desired package state. C(latest) ensures that the latest version is installed. C(build-dep) ensures the package build dependencies\\n32:         are installed. C(fixed) attempt to correct a system with broken dependencies in place.\\n33:     type: str\\n34:     default: present\\n35:     choices: [ absent, build-dep, latest, present, fixed ]\\n36:   update_cache:\\n37:     description:\\n38:       - Run the equivalent of C(apt-get update) before the operation. Can be run as part of the package installation or as a separate step.\\n39:       - Default is not to update the cache.\\n40:     aliases: [ update-cache ]\\n41:     type: bool\\n42:   update_cache_retries:\\n43:     description:\\n44:       - Amount of retries if the cache update fails. Also see I(update_cache_retry_max_delay).\\n45:     type: int\\n46:     default: 5\\n47:     version_added: '2.10'\\n48:   update_cache_retry_max_delay:\\n49:     description:\\n50:       - Use an exponential backoff delay for each retry (see I(update_cache_retries)) up to this max delay in seconds.\\n51:     type: int\\n52:     default: 12\\n53:     version_added: '2.10'\\n54:   cache_valid_time:\\n55:     description:\\n56:       - Update the apt cache if it is older than the I(cache_valid_time). This option is set in seconds.\\n57:       - As of Ansible 2.4, if explicitly set, this sets I(update_cache=yes).\\n58:     type: int\\n59:     default: 0\\n60:   purge:\\n61:     description:\\n62:      - Will force purging of configuration files if the module state is set to I(absent).\\n63:     type: bool\\n64:     default: 'no'\\n65:   default_release:\\n66:     description:\\n67:       - Corresponds to the C(-t) option for I(apt) and sets pin priorities\\n68:     aliases: [ default-release ]\\n69:     type: str\\n70:   install_recommends:\\n71:     description:\\n72:       - Corresponds to the C(--no-install-recommends) option for I(apt). C(yes) installs recommended packages.  C(no) does not install\\n73:         recommended packages. By default, Ansible will use the same defaults as the operating system. Suggested packages are never installed.\\n74:     aliases: [ install-recommends ]\\n75:     type: bool\\n76:   force:\\n77:     description:\\n78:       - 'Corresponds to the C(--force-yes) to I(apt-get) and implies C(allow_unauthenticated: yes)'\\n79:       - \\\"This option will disable checking both the packages' signatures and the certificates of the\\n80:         web servers they are downloaded from.\\\"\\n81:       - 'This option *is not* the equivalent of passing the C(-f) flag to I(apt-get) on the command line'\\n82:       - '**This is a destructive operation with the potential to destroy your system, and it should almost never be used.**\\n83:          Please also see C(man apt-get) for more information.'\\n84:     type: bool\\n85:     default: 'no'\\n86:   allow_unauthenticated:\\n87:     description:\\n88:       - Ignore if packages cannot be authenticated. This is useful for bootstrapping environments that manage their own apt-key setup.\\n89:       - 'C(allow_unauthenticated) is only supported with state: I(install)/I(present)'\\n90:     aliases: [ allow-unauthenticated ]\\n91:     type: bool\\n92:     default: 'no'\\n93:     version_added: \\\"2.1\\\"\\n94:   upgrade:\\n95:     description:\\n96:       - If yes or safe, performs an aptitude safe-upgrade.\\n97:       - If full, performs an aptitude full-upgrade.\\n98:       - If dist, performs an apt-get dist-upgrade.\\n99:       - 'Note: This does not upgrade a specific package, use state=latest for that.'\\n100:       - 'Note: Since 2.4, apt-get is used as a fall-back if aptitude is not present.'\\n101:     version_added: \\\"1.1\\\"\\n102:     choices: [ dist, full, 'no', safe, 'yes' ]\\n103:     default: 'no'\\n104:     type: str\\n105:   dpkg_options:\\n106:     description:\\n107:       - Add dpkg options to apt command. Defaults to '-o \\\"Dpkg::Options::=--force-confdef\\\" -o \\\"Dpkg::Options::=--force-confold\\\"'\\n108:       - Options should be supplied as comma separated list\\n109:     default: force-confdef,force-confold\\n110:     type: str\\n111:   deb:\\n112:      description:\\n113:        - Path to a .deb package on the remote machine.\\n114:        - If :// in the path, ansible will attempt to download deb before installing. (Version added 2.1)\\n115:        - Requires the C(xz-utils) package to extract the control file of the deb package to install.\\n116:      type: path\\n117:      required: false\\n118:      version_added: \\\"1.6\\\"\\n119:   autoremove:\\n120:     description:\\n121:       - If C(yes), remove unused dependency packages for all module states except I(build-dep). It can also be used as the only option.\\n122:       - Previous to version 2.4, autoclean was also an alias for autoremove, now it is its own separate command. See documentation for further information.\\n123:     type: bool\\n124:     default: 'no'\\n125:     version_added: \\\"2.1\\\"\\n126:   autoclean:\\n127:     description:\\n128:       - If C(yes), cleans the local repository of retrieved package files that can no longer be downloaded.\\n129:     type: bool\\n130:     default: 'no'\\n131:     version_added: \\\"2.4\\\"\\n132:   policy_rc_d:\\n133:     description:\\n134:       - Force the exit code of /usr/sbin/policy-rc.d.\\n135:       - For example, if I(policy_rc_d=101) the installed package will not trigger a service start.\\n136:       - If /usr/sbin/policy-rc.d already exists, it is backed up and restored after the package installation.\\n137:       - If C(null), the /usr/sbin/policy-rc.d isn't created/changed.\\n138:     type: int\\n139:     default: null\\n140:     version_added: \\\"2.8\\\"\\n141:   only_upgrade:\\n142:     description:\\n143:       - Only upgrade a package if it is already installed.\\n144:     type: bool\\n145:     default: 'no'\\n146:     version_added: \\\"2.1\\\"\\n147:   fail_on_autoremove:\\n148:     description:\\n149:       - 'Corresponds to the C(--no-remove) option for C(apt).'\\n150:       - 'If C(yes), it is ensured that no packages will be removed or the task will fail.'\\n151: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/ansible--4c5ce5a1@4c5ce5a1a9e79a845aff4978cfeb72a0d4ecf7d6/-/blob/lib/ansible/modules/apt.py%3FL1-150\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: #!/usr/bin/python\n2: # -*- coding: utf-8 -*-\n3: \n4: # Copyright: (c) 2012, Flowroute LLC\n5: # Written by Matthew Williams <matthew@flowroute.com>\n6: # Based on yum module written by Seth Vidal <skvidal at fedoraproject.org>\n7: \n8: # GNU General Public License v3.0+ (see COPYING or https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-3.0.txt)\n9: \n10: from __future__ import absolute_import, division, print_function\n11: __metaclass__ = type\n12: \n13: \n14: DOCUMENTATION = '''\n15: ---\n16: module: apt\n17: short_description: Manages apt-packages\n18: description:\n19:   - Manages I(apt) packages (such as for Debian/Ubuntu).\n20: version_added: \"0.0.2\"\n21: options:\n22:   name:\n23:     description:\n24:       - A list of package names, like C(foo), or package specifier with version, like C(foo=1.0).\n25:         Name wildcards (fnmatch) like C(apt*) and version wildcards like C(foo=1.0*) are also supported.\n26:     aliases: [ package, pkg ]\n27:     type: list\n28:     elements: str\n29:   state:\n30:     description:\n31:       - Indicates the desired package state. C(latest) ensures that the latest version is installed. C(build-dep) ensures the package build dependencies\n32:         are installed. C(fixed) attempt to correct a system with broken dependencies in place.\n33:     type: str\n34:     default: present\n35:     choices: [ absent, build-dep, latest, present, fixed ]\n36:   update_cache:\n37:     description:\n38:       - Run the equivalent of C(apt-get update) before the operation. Can be run as part of the package installation or as a separate step.\n39:       - Default is not to update the cache.\n40:     aliases: [ update-cache ]\n41:     type: bool\n42:   update_cache_retries:\n43:     description:\n44:       - Amount of retries if the cache update fails. Also see I(update_cache_retry_max_delay).\n45:     type: int\n46:     default: 5\n47:     version_added: '2.10'\n48:   update_cache_retry_max_delay:\n49:     description:\n50:       - Use an exponential backoff delay for each retry (see I(update_cache_retries)) up to this max delay in seconds.\n51:     type: int\n52:     default: 12\n53:     version_added: '2.10'\n54:   cache_valid_time:\n55:     description:\n56:       - Update the apt cache if it is older than the I(cache_valid_time). This option is set in seconds.\n57:       - As of Ansible 2.4, if explicitly set, this sets I(update_cache=yes).\n58:     type: int\n59:     default: 0\n60:   purge:\n61:     description:\n62:      - Will force purging of configuration files if the module state is set to I(absent).\n63:     type: bool\n64:     default: 'no'\n65:   default_release:\n66:     description:\n67:       - Corresponds to the C(-t) option for I(apt) and sets pin priorities\n68:     aliases: [ default-release ]\n69:     type: str\n70:   install_recommends:\n71:     description:\n72:       - Corresponds to the C(--no-install-recommends) option for I(apt). C(yes) installs recommended packages.  C(no) does not install\n73:         recommended packages. By default, Ansible will use the same defaults as the operating system. Suggested packages are never installed.\n74:     aliases: [ install-recommends ]\n75:     type: bool\n76:   force:\n77:     description:\n78:       - 'Corresponds to the C(--force-yes) to I(apt-get) and implies C(allow_unauthenticated: yes)'\n79:       - \"This option will disable checking both the packages' signatures and the certificates of the\n80:         web servers they are downloaded from.\"\n81:       - 'This option *is not* the equivalent of passing the C(-f) flag to I(apt-get) on the command line'\n82:       - '**This is a destructive operation with the potential to destroy your system, and it should almost never be used.**\n83:          Please also see C(man apt-get) for more information.'\n84:     type: bool\n85:     default: 'no'\n86:   allow_unauthenticated:\n87:     description:\n88:       - Ignore if packages cannot be authenticated. This is useful for bootstrapping environments that manage their own apt-key setup.\n89:       - 'C(allow_unauthenticated) is only supported with state: I(install)/I(present)'\n90:     aliases: [ allow-unauthenticated ]\n91:     type: bool\n92:     default: 'no'\n93:     version_added: \"2.1\"\n94:   upgrade:\n95:     description:\n96:       - If yes or safe, performs an aptitude safe-upgrade.\n97:       - If full, performs an aptitude full-upgrade.\n98:       - If dist, performs an apt-get dist-upgrade.\n99:       - 'Note: This does not upgrade a specific package, use state=latest for that.'\n100:       - 'Note: Since 2.4, apt-get is used as a fall-back if aptitude is not present.'\n101:     version_added: \"1.1\"\n102:     choices: [ dist, full, 'no', safe, 'yes' ]\n103:     default: 'no'\n104:     type: str\n105:   dpkg_options:\n106:     description:\n107:       - Add dpkg options to apt command. Defaults to '-o \"Dpkg::Options::=--force-confdef\" -o \"Dpkg::Options::=--force-confold\"'\n108:       - Options should be supplied as comma separated list\n109:     default: force-confdef,force-confold\n110:     type: str\n111:   deb:\n112:      description:\n113:        - Path to a .deb package on the remote machine.\n114:        - If :// in the path, ansible will attempt to download deb before installing. (Version added 2.1)\n115:        - Requires the C(xz-utils) package to extract the control file of the deb package to install.\n116:      type: path\n117:      required: false\n118:      version_added: \"1.6\"\n119:   autoremove:\n120:     description:\n121:       - If C(yes), remove unused dependency packages for all module states except I(build-dep). It can also be used as the only option.\n122:       - Previous to version 2.4, autoclean was also an alias for autoremove, now it is its own separate command. See documentation for further information.\n123:     type: bool\n124:     default: 'no'\n125:     version_added: \"2.1\"\n126:   autoclean:\n127:     description:\n128:       - If C(yes), cleans the local repository of retrieved package files that can no longer be downloaded.\n129:     type: bool\n130:     default: 'no'\n131:     version_added: \"2.4\"\n132:   policy_rc_d:\n133:     description:\n134:       - Force the exit code of /usr/sbin/policy-rc.d.\n135:       - For example, if I(policy_rc_d=101) the installed package will not trigger a service start.\n136:       - If /usr/sbin/policy-rc.d already exists, it is backed up and restored after the package installation.\n137:       - If C(null), the /usr/sbin/policy-rc.d isn't created/changed.\n138:     type: int\n139:     default: null\n140:     version_added: \"2.8\"\n141:   only_upgrade:\n142:     description:\n143:       - Only upgrade a package if it is already installed.\n144:     type: bool\n145:     default: 'no'\n146:     version_added: \"2.1\"\n147:   fail_on_autoremove:\n148:     description:\n149:       - 'Corresponds to the C(--no-remove) option for C(apt).'\n150:       - 'If C(yes), it is ensured that no packages will be removed or the task will fail.'\n151: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/ansible--4c5ce5a1@4c5ce5a1a9e79a845aff4978cfeb72a0d4ecf7d6/-/blob/lib/ansible/modules/apt.py%3FL1-150"
  }
}

Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1100:         #    system locations)\\n1101: \\n1102:         apt_pkg_name = 'python3-apt' if PY3 else 'python-apt'\\n1103: \\n1104:         if has_respawned():\\n1105:             # this shouldn't be possible; short-circuit early if it happens...\\n1106:             module.fail_json(msg=\\\"{0} must be installed and visible from {1}.\\\".format(apt_pkg_name, sys.executable))\\n1107: \\n1108:         interpreters = ['/usr/bin/python3', '/usr/bin/python2', '/usr/bin/python']\\n1109: \\n1110:         interpreter = probe_interpreters_for_module(interpreters, 'apt')\\n1111: \\n1112:         if interpreter:\\n1113:             # found the Python bindings; respawn this module under the interpreter where we found them\\n1114:             respawn_module(interpreter)\\n1115:             # this is the end of the line for this process, it will exit here once the respawned module has completed\\n1116: \\n1117:         # don't make changes if we're in check_mode\\n1118:         if module.check_mode:\\n1119:             module.fail_json(msg=\\\"%s must be installed to use check mode. \\\"\\n1120:                                  \\\"If run normally this module can auto-install it.\\\" % apt_pkg_name)\\n1121: \\n1122:         # We skip cache update in auto install the dependency if the\\n1123:         # user explicitly declared it with update_cache=no.\\n1124:         if module.params.get('update_cache') is False:\\n1125:             module.warn(\\\"Auto-installing missing dependency without updating cache: %s\\\" % apt_pkg_name)\\n1126:         else:\\n1127:             module.warn(\\\"Updating cache and auto-installing missing dependency: %s\\\" % apt_pkg_name)\\n1128:             module.run_command(['apt-get', 'update'], check_rc=True)\\n1129: \\n1130:         # try to install the apt Python binding\\n1131:         module.run_command(['apt-get', 'install', '--no-install-recommends', apt_pkg_name, '-y', '-q'], check_rc=True)\\n1132: \\n1133:         # try again to find the bindings in common places\\n1134:         interpreter = probe_interpreters_for_module(interpreters, 'apt')\\n1135: \\n1136:         if interpreter:\\n1137:             # found the Python bindings; respawn this module under the interpreter where we found them\\n1138:             # NB: respawn is somewhat wasteful if it's this interpreter, but simplifies the code\\n1139:             respawn_module(interpreter)\\n1140:             # this is the end of the line for this process, it will exit here once the respawned module has completed\\n1141:         else:\\n1142:             # we've done all we can do; just tell the user it's busted and get out\\n1143:             module.fail_json(msg=\\\"{0} must be installed and visible from {1}.\\\".format(apt_pkg_name, sys.executable))\\n1144: \\n1145:     global APTITUDE_CMD\\n1146:     APTITUDE_CMD = module.get_bin_path(\\\"aptitude\\\", False)\\n1147:     global APT_GET_CMD\\n1148:     APT_GET_CMD = module.get_bin_path(\\\"apt-get\\\")\\n1149: \\n1150:     p = module.params\\n1151: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/ansible--4c5ce5a1@4c5ce5a1a9e79a845aff4978cfeb72a0d4ecf7d6/-/blob/lib/ansible/modules/apt.py%3FL1100-1150\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1100:         #    system locations)\n1101: \n1102:         apt_pkg_name = 'python3-apt' if PY3 else 'python-apt'\n1103: \n1104:         if has_respawned():\n1105:             # this shouldn't be possible; short-circuit early if it happens...\n1106:             module.fail_json(msg=\"{0} must be installed and visible from {1}.\".format(apt_pkg_name, sys.executable))\n1107: \n1108:         interpreters = ['/usr/bin/python3', '/usr/bin/python2', '/usr/bin/python']\n1109: \n1110:         interpreter = probe_interpreters_for_module(interpreters, 'apt')\n1111: \n1112:         if interpreter:\n1113:             # found the Python bindings; respawn this module under the interpreter where we found them\n1114:             respawn_module(interpreter)\n1115:             # this is the end of the line for this process, it will exit here once the respawned module has completed\n1116: \n1117:         # don't make changes if we're in check_mode\n1118:         if module.check_mode:\n1119:             module.fail_json(msg=\"%s must be installed to use check mode. \"\n1120:                                  \"If run normally this module can auto-install it.\" % apt_pkg_name)\n1121: \n1122:         # We skip cache update in auto install the dependency if the\n1123:         # user explicitly declared it with update_cache=no.\n1124:         if module.params.get('update_cache') is False:\n1125:             module.warn(\"Auto-installing missing dependency without updating cache: %s\" % apt_pkg_name)\n1126:         else:\n1127:             module.warn(\"Updating cache and auto-installing missing dependency: %s\" % apt_pkg_name)\n1128:             module.run_command(['apt-get', 'update'], check_rc=True)\n1129: \n1130:         # try to install the apt Python binding\n1131:         module.run_command(['apt-get', 'install', '--no-install-recommends', apt_pkg_name, '-y', '-q'], check_rc=True)\n1132: \n1133:         # try again to find the bindings in common places\n1134:         interpreter = probe_interpreters_for_module(interpreters, 'apt')\n1135: \n1136:         if interpreter:\n1137:             # found the Python bindings; respawn this module under the interpreter where we found them\n1138:             # NB: respawn is somewhat wasteful if it's this interpreter, but simplifies the code\n1139:             respawn_module(interpreter)\n1140:             # this is the end of the line for this process, it will exit here once the respawned module has completed\n1141:         else:\n1142:             # we've done all we can do; just tell the user it's busted and get out\n1143:             module.fail_json(msg=\"{0} must be installed and visible from {1}.\".format(apt_pkg_name, sys.executable))\n1144: \n1145:     global APTITUDE_CMD\n1146:     APTITUDE_CMD = module.get_bin_path(\"aptitude\", False)\n1147:     global APT_GET_CMD\n1148:     APT_GET_CMD = module.get_bin_path(\"apt-get\")\n1149: \n1150:     p = module.params\n1151: ",

  }

Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"500:         r1 = str(r1)\\n501:         if e2 is None:\\n502:             e2 = '0'\\n503:         else:\\n504:             e2 = str(e2)\\n505:         v2 = str(v2)\\n506:         r2 = str(r2)\\n507:         # print '%s, %s, %s vs %s, %s, %s' % (e1, v1, r1, e2, v2, r2)\\n508:         rc = dnf.rpm.rpm.labelCompare((e1, v1, r1), (e2, v2, r2))\\n509:         # print '%s, %s, %s vs %s, %s, %s = %s' % (e1, v1, r1, e2, v2, r2, rc)\\n510:         return rc\\n511: \\n512:     def _ensure_dnf(self):\\n513:         if HAS_DNF:\\n514:             return\\n515: \\n516:         system_interpreters = ['/usr/libexec/platform-python',\\n517:                                '/usr/bin/python3',\\n518:                                '/usr/bin/python2',\\n519:                                '/usr/bin/python']\\n520: \\n521:         if not has_respawned():\\n522:             # probe well-known system Python locations for accessible bindings, favoring py3\\n523:             interpreter = probe_interpreters_for_module(system_interpreters, 'dnf')\\n524: \\n525:             if interpreter:\\n526:                 # respawn under the interpreter where the bindings should be found\\n527:                 respawn_module(interpreter)\\n528:                 # end of the line for this module, the process will exit here once the respawned module completes\\n529: \\n530:         # done all we can do, something is just broken (auto-install isn't useful anymore with respawn, so it was removed)\\n531:         self.module.fail_json(\\n532:             msg=\\\"Could not import the dnf python module using {0} ({1}). \\\"\\n533:                 \\\"Please install `python3-dnf` or `python2-dnf` package or ensure you have specified the \\\"\\n534:                 \\\"correct ansible_python_interpreter. (attempted {2})\\\"\\n535:                 .format(sys.executable, sys.version.replace('\\\\n', ''), system_interpreters),\\n536:             results=[]\\n537:         )\\n538: \\n539:     def _configure_base(self, base, conf_file, disable_gpg_check, installroot='/'):\\n540:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Configure the dnf Base object.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n541: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/ansible--4c5ce5a1@4c5ce5a1a9e79a845aff4978cfeb72a0d4ecf7d6/-/blob/lib/ansible/modules/dnf.py%3FL500-540\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "500:         r1 = str(r1)\n501:         if e2 is None:\n502:             e2 = '0'\n503:         else:\n504:             e2 = str(e2)\n505:         v2 = str(v2)\n506:         r2 = str(r2)\n507:         # print '%s, %s, %s vs %s, %s, %s' % (e1, v1, r1, e2, v2, r2)\n508:         rc = dnf.rpm.rpm.labelCompare((e1, v1, r1), (e2, v2, r2))\n509:         # print '%s, %s, %s vs %s, %s, %s = %s' % (e1, v1, r1, e2, v2, r2, rc)\n510:         return rc\n511: \n512:     def _ensure_dnf(self):\n513:         if HAS_DNF:\n514:             return\n515: \n516:         system_interpreters = ['/usr/libexec/platform-python',\n517:                                '/usr/bin/python3',\n518:                                '/usr/bin/python2',\n519:                                '/usr/bin/python']\n520: \n521:         if not has_respawned():\n522:             # probe well-known system Python locations for accessible bindings, favoring py3\n523:             interpreter = probe_interpreters_for_module(system_interpreters, 'dnf')\n524: \n525:             if interpreter:\n526:                 # respawn under the interpreter where the bindings should be found\n527:                 respawn_module(interpreter)\n528:                 # end of the line for this module, the process will exit here once the respawned module completes\n529: \n530:         # done all we can do, something is just broken (auto-install isn't useful anymore with respawn, so it was removed)\n531:         self.module.fail_json(\n532:             msg=\"Could not import the dnf python module using {0} ({1}). \"\n533:                 \"Please install `python3-dnf` or `python2-dnf` package or ensure you have specified the \"\n534:                 \"correct ansible_python_interpreter. (attempted {2})\"\n535:                 .format(sys.executable, sys.version.replace('\\n', ''), system_interpreters),\n536:             results=[]\n537:         )\n538: \n539:     def _configure_base(self, base, conf_file, disable_gpg_check, installroot='/'):\n540:         \"\"\"Configure the dnf Base object.\"\"\"\n541: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/ansible--4c5ce5a1@4c5ce5a1a9e79a845aff4978cfeb72a0d4ecf7d6/-/blob/lib/ansible/modules/dnf.py%3FL500-540"
  }

34. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": "changelogs/fragments/module_respawn.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/ansible--4c5ce5a1"
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: minor_changes:\\n2: - Module API - new module_respawn API allows modules that need to run under a specific Python interpreter to respawn in place under that interpreter\\n3: - Module API - libselinux-python is no longer required for basic module API selinux operations (affects core modules assemble, blockinfile, copy, cron, file, get_url, lineinfile, setup, replace, unarchive, uri, user, yum_repository)\\n4: - apt - module now works under any supported Python interpreter\\n5: - apt_repository - module now works under any supported Python interpreter\\n6: - dnf - module now works under any supported Python interpreter\\n7: - package_facts - module support for apt and rpm now works under any supported Python interpreter\\n8: - yum - module now works under any supported Python interpreter\\n9: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/ansible--4c5ce5a1@4c5ce5a1a9e79a845aff4978cfeb72a0d4ecf7d6/-/blob/changelogs/fragments/module_respawn.yml\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: minor_changes:\n2: - Module API - new module_respawn API allows modules that need to run under a specific Python interpreter to respawn in place under that interpreter\n3: - Module API - libselinux-python is no longer required for basic module API selinux operations (affects core modules assemble, blockinfile, copy, cron, file, get_url, lineinfile, setup, replace, unarchive, uri, user, yum_repository)\n4: - apt - module now works under any supported Python interpreter\n5: - apt_repository - module now works under any supported Python interpreter\n6: - dnf - module now works under any supported Python interpreter\n7: - package_facts - module support for apt and rpm now works under any supported Python interpreter\n8: - yum - module now works under any supported Python interpreter\n9: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/ansible--4c5ce5a1@4c5ce5a1a9e79a845aff4978cfeb72a0d4ecf7d6/-/blob/changelogs/fragments/module_respawn.yml"
  }
}
35. Bash @ -

43. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "# Copyright: (c) 2021, Ansible Project\n# GNU General Public License v3.0+ (see COPYING or https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-3.0.txt)\n\nfrom __future__ import (absolute_import, division, print_function)\n__metaclass__ = type\n\nimport os\nimport subprocess\nimport sys\n\nfrom ansible.module_utils.common.text.converters import to_bytes, to_native\n\n\ndef has_respawned():\n    return hasattr(sys.modules['__main__'], '_respawned')\n\n\ndef respawn_module(interpreter_path):\n    \"\"\"\n    Respawn the currently-running Ansible Python module under the specified Python interpreter.\n\n    Ansible modules that require libraries that are typically available only under well-known interpreters\n    (eg, ``yum``, ``apt``, ``dnf``) can use bespoke logic to determine the libraries they need are not\n    available, then call `respawn_module` to re-execute the current module under a different interpreter\n    and exit the current process when the new subprocess has completed. The respawned process inherits only\n    stdout/stderr from the current process.\n\n    Only a single respawn is allowed. ``respawn_module`` will fail on nested respawns. Modules are encouraged\n    to call `has_respawned()` to defensively guide behavior before calling ``respawn_module``, and to ensure\n    that the target interpreter exists, as ``respawn_module`` will not fail gracefully.\n\n    :arg interpreter_path: path to a Python interpreter to respawn the current module\n    \"\"\"\n\n    if has_respawned():\n        raise Exception('module has already been respawned')\n\n    # FUTURE: we need a safe way to log that a respawn has occurred for forensic/debug purposes\n    payload = _create_payload()\n    stdin_read, stdin_write = os.pipe()\n    os.write(stdin_write, to_bytes(payload))\n    os.close(stdin_write)\n    rc = subprocess.call([interpreter_path, '--'], stdin=stdin_read)\n    sys.exit(rc)  # pylint: disable=ansible-bad-function\n\n\ndef probe_interpreters_for_module(interpreter_paths, module_name):\n    \"\"\"\n    Probes a supplied list of Python interpreters, returning the first one capable of\n    importing the named module. This is useful when attempting to locate a \"system\n    Python\" where OS-packaged utility modules are located.\n\n    :arg interpreter_paths: iterable of paths to Python interpreters. The paths will be probed\n    in order, and the first path that exists and can successfully import the named module will\n    be returned (or ``None`` if probing fails for all supplied paths).\n    :arg module_name: fully-qualified Python module name to probe for (eg, ``selinux``)\n    \"\"\"\n    for interpreter_path in interpreter_paths:\n        if not os.path.exists(interpreter_path):\n            continue\n        try:\n            rc = subprocess.call([interpreter_path, '-c', 'import {0}'.format(module_name)])\n            if rc == 0:\n                return interpreter_path\n        except Exception:\n            continue\n\n    return None\n\n\ndef _create_payload():\n    from ansible.module_utils import basic\n    smuggled_args = getattr(basic, '_ANSIBLE_ARGS')\n    if not smuggled_args:\n        raise Exception('unable to access ansible.module_utils.basic._ANSIBLE_ARGS (not launched by AnsiballZ?)')\n    module_fqn = sys.modules['__main__']._module_fqn\n    modlib_path = sys.modules['__main__']._modlib_path\n    respawn_code_template = '''\\\nimport runpy\nimport sys\n\nmodule_fqn = '{module_fqn}'\nmodlib_path = '{modlib_path}'\nsmuggled_args = b\"\"\"{smuggled_args}\"\"\".strip()\n\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    sys.path.insert(0, modlib_path)\n\n    from ansible.module_utils import basic\n    basic._ANSIBLE_ARGS = smuggled_args\n\n    runpy.run_module(module_fqn, init_globals=dict(_respawned=True), run_name='__main__', alter_sys=True)\n    '''\n\n    respawn_code = respawn_code_template.format(module_fqn=module_fqn, modlib_path=modlib_path, smuggled_args=to_native(smuggled_args))\n\n    return respawn_code\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/lib/ansible/module_utils/common/respawn.py"
}
Output
{
  "content": "# Copyright: (c) 2021, Ansible Project\n# GNU General Public License v3.0+ (see COPYING or https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-3.0.txt)\n\nfrom __future__ import (absolute_import, division, print_function)\n__metaclass__ = type\n\nimport os\nimport subprocess\nimport sys\n\nfrom ansible.module_utils.common.text.converters import to_bytes, to_native\n\n\ndef has_respawned():\n    return hasattr(sys.modules['__main__'], '_respawned')\n\n\ndef respawn_module(interpreter_path):\n    \"\"\"\n    Respawn the currently-running Ansible Python module under the specified Python interpreter.\n\n    Ansible modules that require libraries that are typically available only under well-known interpreters\n    (eg, ``yum``, ``apt``, ``dnf``) can use bespoke logic to determine the libraries they need are not\n    available, then call `respawn_module` to re-execute the current module under a different interpreter\n    and exit the current process when the new subprocess has completed. The respawned process inherits only\n    stdout/stderr from the current process.\n\n    Only a single respawn is allowed. ``respawn_module`` will fail on nested respawns. Modules are encouraged\n    to call `has_respawned()` to defensively guide behavior before calling ``respawn_module``, and to ensure\n    that the target interpreter exists, as ``respawn_module`` will not fail gracefully.\n\n    :arg interpreter_path: path to a Python interpreter to respawn the current module\n    \"\"\"\n\n    if has_respawned():\n        raise Exception('module has already been respawned')\n\n    # FUTURE: we need a safe way to log that a respawn has occurred for forensic/debug purposes\n    payload = _create_payload()\n    stdin_read, stdin_write = os.pipe()\n    os.write(stdin_write, to_bytes(payload))\n    os.close(stdin_write)\n    rc = subprocess.call([interpreter_path, '--'], stdin=stdin_read)\n    sys.exit(rc)  # pylint: disable=ansible-bad-function\n\n\ndef probe_interpreters_for_module(interpreter_paths, module_name):\n    \"\"\"\n    Probes a supplied list of Python interpreters, returning the first one capable of\n    importing the named module. This is useful when attempting to locate a \"system\n    Python\" where OS-packaged utility modules are located.\n\n    :arg interpreter_paths: iterable of paths to Python interpreters. The paths will be probed\n    in order, and the first path that exists and can successfully import the named module will\n    be returned (or ``None`` if probing fails for all supplied paths).\n    :arg module_name: fully-qualified Python module name to probe for (eg, ``selinux``)\n    \"\"\"\n    for interpreter_path in interpreter_paths:\n        if not os.path.exists(interpreter_path):\n            continue\n        try:\n            rc = subprocess.call([interpreter_path, '-c', 'import {0}'.format(module_name)])\n            if rc == 0:\n                return interpreter_path\n        except Exception:\n            continue\n\n    return None\n\n\ndef _create_payload():\n    from ansible.module_utils import basic\n    smuggled_args = getattr(basic, '_ANSIBLE_ARGS')\n    if not smuggled_args:\n        raise Exception('unable to access ansible.module_utils.basic._ANSIBLE_ARGS (not launched by AnsiballZ?)')\n    module_fqn = sys.modules['__main__']._module_fqn\n    modlib_path = sys.modules['__main__']._modlib_path\n    respawn_code_template = '''\\\nimport runpy\nimport sys\n\nmodule_fqn = '{module_fqn}'\nmodlib_path = '{modlib_path}'\nsmuggled_args = b\"\"\"{smuggled_args}\"\"\".strip()\n\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    sys.path.insert(0, modlib_path)\n\n    from ansible.module_utils import basic\n    basic._ANSIBLE_ARGS = smuggled_args\n\n    runpy.run_module(module_fqn, init_globals=dict(_respawned=True), run_name='__main__', alter_sys=True)\n    '''\n\n    respawn_code = respawn_code_template.format(module_fqn=module_fqn, modlib_path=modlib_path, smuggled_args=to_native(smuggled_args))\n\n    return respawn_code\n",
  "filePath": "/workspace/lib/ansible/module_utils/common/respawn.py",

44. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "# Copyright: (c) 2021, Ansible Project\n# GNU General Public License v3.0+ (see COPYING or https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-3.0.txt)\n\nfrom __future__ import (absolute_import, division, print_function)\n__metaclass__ = type\n\nimport os\nimport sys\n\nfrom ansible.module_utils.common.text.converters import to_native, to_bytes\nfrom ctypes import CDLL, c_char_p, c_int, byref, POINTER, get_errno\n\ntry:\n    _selinux_lib = CDLL('libselinux.so.1', use_errno=True)\nexcept OSError:\n    raise ImportError('unable to load libselinux.so')\n\n\ndef _module_setup():\n    def _check_rc(rc):\n        if rc < 0:\n            errno = get_errno()\n            raise OSError(errno, os.strerror(errno))\n        return rc\n\n    binary_char_type = type(b'')\n\n    class _to_char_p:\n        @classmethod\n        def from_param(cls, strvalue):\n            if strvalue is not None and not isinstance(strvalue, binary_char_type):\n                strvalue = to_bytes(strvalue)\n\n            return strvalue\n\n    # FIXME: swap restype to errcheck\n\n    _funcmap = dict(\n        is_selinux_enabled={},\n        is_selinux_mls_enabled={},\n        lgetfilecon_raw=dict(argtypes=[_to_char_p, POINTER(c_char_p)], restype=_check_rc),\n        # NB: matchpathcon is deprecated and should be rewritten on selabel_lookup (but will be a PITA)\n        matchpathcon=dict(argtypes=[_to_char_p, c_int, POINTER(c_char_p)], restype=_check_rc),\n        security_policyvers={},\n        selinux_getenforcemode=dict(argtypes=[POINTER(c_int)]),\n        security_getenforce={},\n        lsetfilecon=dict(argtypes=[_to_char_p, _to_char_p], restype=_check_rc)\n    )\n\n    _thismod = sys.modules[__name__]\n\n    for fname, cfg in _funcmap.items():\n        fn = getattr(_selinux_lib, fname, None)\n\n        if not fn:\n            raise ImportError('missing selinux function: {0}'.format(fname))\n\n        # all ctypes pointers share the same base type\n        base_ptr_type = type(POINTER(c_int))\n        fn.argtypes = cfg.get('argtypes', None)\n        fn.restype = cfg.get('restype', c_int)\n\n        # just patch simple directly callable functions directly onto the module\n        if not fn.argtypes or not any(argtype for argtype in fn.argtypes if type(argtype) == base_ptr_type):\n            setattr(_thismod, fname, fn)\n            continue\n\n    # NB: this validation code must run after all the wrappers have been declared\n    unimplemented_funcs = set(_funcmap).difference(dir(_thismod))\n    if unimplemented_funcs:\n        raise NotImplementedError('implementation is missing functions: {0}'.format(unimplemented_funcs))\n\n\n# begin wrapper function impls\n\ndef selinux_getenforcemode():\n    enforcemode = c_int()\n    rc = _selinux_lib.selinux_getenforcemode(byref(enforcemode))\n    return [rc, enforcemode.value]\n\n\ndef lgetfilecon_raw(path):\n    con = c_char_p()\n    try:\n        rc = _selinux_lib.lgetfilecon_raw(path, byref(con))\n        return [rc, to_native(con.value)]\n    finally:\n        _selinux_lib.freecon(con)\n\n\ndef matchpathcon(path, mode):\n    con = c_char_p()\n    try:\n        rc = _selinux_lib.matchpathcon(path, mode, byref(con))\n        return [rc, to_native(con.value)]\n    finally:\n        _selinux_lib.freecon(con)\n\n\n_module_setup()\ndel _module_setup\n\n# end wrapper function impls\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/lib/ansible/module_utils/compat/selinux.py"
}
Output
{
  "content": "# Copyright: (c) 2021, Ansible Project\n# GNU General Public License v3.0+ (see COPYING or https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-3.0.txt)\n\nfrom __future__ import (absolute_import, division, print_function)\n__metaclass__ = type\n\nimport os\nimport sys\n\nfrom ansible.module_utils.common.text.converters import to_native, to_bytes\nfrom ctypes import CDLL, c_char_p, c_int, byref, POINTER, get_errno\n\ntry:\n    _selinux_lib = CDLL('libselinux.so.1', use_errno=True)\nexcept OSError:\n    raise ImportError('unable to load libselinux.so')\n\n\ndef _module_setup():\n    def _check_rc(rc):\n        if rc < 0:\n            errno = get_errno()\n            raise OSError(errno, os.strerror(errno))\n        return rc\n\n    binary_char_type = type(b'')\n\n    class _to_char_p:\n        @classmethod\n        def from_param(cls, strvalue):\n            if strvalue is not None and not isinstance(strvalue, binary_char_type):\n                strvalue = to_bytes(strvalue)\n\n            return strvalue\n\n    # FIXME: swap restype to errcheck\n\n    _funcmap = dict(\n        is_selinux_enabled={},\n        is_selinux_mls_enabled={},\n        lgetfilecon_raw=dict(argtypes=[_to_char_p, POINTER(c_char_p)], restype=_check_rc),\n        # NB: matchpathcon is deprecated and should be rewritten on selabel_lookup (but will be a PITA)\n        matchpathcon=dict(argtypes=[_to_char_p, c_int, POINTER(c_char_p)], restype=_check_rc),\n        security_policyvers={},\n        selinux_getenforcemode=dict(argtypes=[POINTER(c_int)]),\n        security_getenforce={},\n        lsetfilecon=dict(argtypes=[_to_char_p, _to_char_p], restype=_check_rc)\n    )\n\n    _thismod = sys.modules[__name__]\n\n    for fname, cfg in _funcmap.items():\n        fn = getattr(_selinux_lib, fname, None)\n\n        if not fn:\n            raise ImportError('missing selinux function: {0}'.format(fname))\n\n        # all ctypes pointers share the same base type\n        base_ptr_type = type(POINTER(c_int))\n        fn.argtypes = cfg.get('argtypes', None)\n        fn.restype = cfg.get('restype', c_int)\n\n        # just patch simple directly callable functions directly onto the module\n        if not fn.argtypes or not any(argtype for argtype in fn.argtypes if type(argtype) == base_ptr_type):\n            setattr(_thismod, fname, fn)\n            continue\n\n    # NB: this validation code must run after all the wrappers have been declared\n    unimplemented_funcs = set(_funcmap).difference(dir(_thismod))\n    if unimplemented_funcs:\n        raise NotImplementedError('implementation is missing functions: {0}'.format(unimplemented_funcs))\n\n\n# begin wrapper function impls\n\ndef selinux_getenforcemode():\n    enforcemode = c_int()\n    rc = _selinux_lib.selinux_getenforcemode(byref(enforcemode))\n    return [rc, enforcemode.value]\n\n\ndef lgetfilecon_raw(path):\n    con = c_char_p()\n    try:\n        rc = _selinux_lib.lgetfilecon_raw(path, byref(con))\n        return [rc, to_native(con.value)]\n    finally:\n        _selinux_lib.freecon(con)\n\n\ndef matchpathcon(path, mode):\n    con = c_char_p()\n    try:\n        rc = _selinux_lib.matchpathcon(path, mode, byref(con))\n        return [rc, to_native(con.value)]\n    finally:\n        _selinux_lib.freecon(con)\n\n\n_module_setup()\ndel _module_setup\n\n# end wrapper function impls\n",
  "filePath": "/workspace/lib/ansible/module_utils/compat/selinux.py",
  "originalFile": null,
  "structuredPatch": [],
  "type": "create"
}
45. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "find test -name \"*respawn*\" -o -name \"*selinux*\" | sort"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "test/integration/targets/copy/tasks/selinux.yml\ntest/integration/targets/file/tasks/selinux_tests.yml\ntest/integration/targets/subversion/roles/subversion/tasks/setup_selinux.yml\ntest/units/module_utils/basic/test_selinux.py"
}
46. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/test/units/module_utils/basic/test_selinux.py"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-\n# (c) 2012-2014, Michael DeHaan <michael.dehaan@gmail.com>\n# (c) 2016 Toshio Kuratomi <tkuratomi@ansible.com>\n# (c) 2017 Ansible Project\n# GNU General Public License v3.0+ (see COPYING or https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-3.0.txt)\n\nfrom __future__ import absolute_import, division, print_function\n__metaclass__ = type\n\nimport errno\nimport json\n\nfrom units.mock.procenv import ModuleTestCase, swap_stdin_and_argv\n\nfrom units.compat.mock import patch, MagicMock, mock_open, Mock\nfrom ansible.module_utils.six.moves import builtins\n\nrealimport = builtins.__import__\n\n\nclass TestSELinux(ModuleTestCase):\n    def test_module_utils_basic_ansible_module_selinux_mls_enabled(self):\n        from ansible.module_utils import basic\n        basic._ANSIBLE_ARGS = None\n\n        am = basic.AnsibleModule(\n            argument_spec=dict(),\n        )\n\n        basic.HAVE_SELINUX = False\n        self.assertEqual(am.selinux_mls_enabled(), False)\n\n        basic.HAVE_SELINUX = True\n        basic.selinux = Mock()\n        with patch.dict('sys.modules', {'selinux': basic.selinux}):\n            with patch('selinux.is_selinux_mls_enabled', return_value=0):\n                self.assertEqual(am.selinux_mls_enabled(), False)\n            with patch('selinux.is_selinux_mls_enabled', return_value=1):\n                self.assertEqual(am.selinux_mls_enabled(), True)\n        delattr(basic, 'selinux')\n\n    def test_module_utils_basic_ansible_module_selinux_initial_context(self):\n        from ansible.module_utils import basic\n        basic._ANSIBLE_ARGS = None\n\n        am = basic.AnsibleModule(\n            argument_spec=dict(),\n        )\n\n        am.selinux_mls_enabled = MagicMock()\n        am.selinux_mls_enabled.return_value = False\n        self.assertEqual(am.selinux_initial_context(), [None, None, None])\n        am.selinux_mls_enabled.return_value = True\n        self.assertEqual(am.selinux_initial_context(), [None, None, None, None])\n\n    def test_module_utils_basic_ansible_module_selinux_enabled(self):\n        from ansible.module_utils import basic\n        basic._ANSIBLE_ARGS = None\n\n        am = basic.AnsibleModule(\n            argument_spec=dict(),\n        )\n\n        # we first test the cases where the python selinux lib is\n        # not installed, which has two paths: one in which the system\n        # does have selinux installed (and the selinuxenabled command\n        # is present and returns 0 when run), or selinux is not installed\n        basic.HAVE_SELINUX = False\n        am.get_bin_path = MagicMock()\n        am.get_bin_path.return_value = '/path/to/selinuxenabled'\n        am.run_command = MagicMock()\n        am.run_command.return_value = (0, '', '')\n        self.assertRaises(SystemExit, am.selinux_enabled)\n        am.get_bin_path.return_value = None\n        self.assertEqual(am.selinux_enabled(), False)\n\n        # finally we test the case where the python selinux lib is installed,\n        # and both possibilities there (enabled vs. disabled)\n        basic.HAVE_SELINUX = True\n        basic.selinux = Mock()\n        with patch.dict('sys.modules', {'selinux': basic.selinux}):\n            with patch('selinux.is_selinux_enabled', return_value=0):\n                self.assertEqual(am.selinux_enabled(), False)\n            with patch('selinux.is_selinux_enabled', return_value=1):\n                self.assertEqual(am.selinux_enabled(), True)\n        delattr(basic, 'selinux')\n\n    def test_module_utils_basic_ansible_module_selinux_default_context(self):\n        from ansible.module_utils import basic\n        basic._ANSIBLE_ARGS = None\n\n        am = basic.AnsibleModule(\n            argument_spec=dict(),\n        )\n\n        am.selinux_initial_context = MagicMock(return_value=[None, None, None, None])\n        am.selinux_enabled = MagicMock(return_value=True)\n\n        # we first test the cases where the python selinux lib is not installed\n        basic.HAVE_SELINUX = False\n        self.assertEqual(am.selinux_default_context(path='/foo/bar'), [None, None, None, None])\n\n        # all following tests assume the python selinux bindings are installed\n        basic.HAVE_SELINUX = True\n\n        basic.selinux = Mock()\n\n        with patch.dict('sys.modules', {'selinux': basic.selinux}):\n            # next, we test with a mocked implementation of selinux.matchpathcon to simulate\n            # an actual context being found\n            with patch('selinux.matchpathcon', return_value=[0, 'unconfined_u:object_r:default_t:s0']):\n                self.assertEqual(am.selinux_default_context(path='/foo/bar'), ['unconfined_u', 'object_r', 'default_t', 's0'])\n\n            # we also test the case where matchpathcon returned a failure\n            with patch('selinux.matchpathcon', return_value=[-1, '']):\n                self.assertEqual(am.selinux_default_context(path='/foo/bar'), [None, None, None, None])\n\n            # finally, we test where an OSError occurred during matchpathcon's call\n            with patch('selinux.matchpathcon', side_effect=OSError):\n                self.assertEqual(am.selinux_default_context(path='/foo/bar'), [None, None, None, None])\n\n        delattr(basic, 'selinux')\n\n    def test_module_utils_basic_ansible_module_selinux_context(self):\n        from ansible.module_utils import basic\n        basic._ANSIBLE_ARGS = None\n\n        am = basic.AnsibleModule(\n            argument_spec=dict(),\n        )\n\n        am.selinux_initial_context = MagicMock(return_value=[None, None, None, None])\n        am.selinux_enabled = MagicMock(return_value=True)\n\n        # we first test the cases where the python selinux lib is not installed\n        basic.HAVE_SELINUX = False\n        self.assertEqual(am.selinux_context(path='/foo/bar'), [None, None, None, None])\n\n        # all following tests assume the python selinux bindings are installed\n        basic.HAVE_SELINUX = True\n\n        basic.selinux = Mock()\n\n        with patch.dict('sys.modules', {'selinux': basic.selinux}):\n            # next, we test with a mocked implementation of selinux.lgetfilecon_raw to simulate\n            # an actual context being found\n            with patch('selinux.lgetfilecon_raw', return_value=[0, 'unconfined_u:object_r:default_t:s0']):\n                self.assertEqual(am.selinux_context(path='/foo/bar'), ['unconfined_u', 'object_r', 'default_t', 's0'])\n\n            # we also test the case where matchpathcon returned a failure\n            with patch('selinux.lgetfilecon_raw', return_value=[-1, '']):\n                self.assertEqual(am.selinux_context(path='/foo/bar'), [None, None, None, None])\n\n            # finally, we test where an OSError occurred during matchpathcon's call\n            e = OSError()\n            e.errno = errno.ENOENT\n            with patch('selinux.lgetfilecon_raw', side_effect=e):\n                self.assertRaises(SystemExit, am.selinux_context, path='/foo/bar')\n\n            e = OSError()\n            with patch('selinux.lgetfilecon_raw', side_effect=e):\n                self.assertRaises(SystemExit, am.selinux_context, path='/foo/bar')\n\n        delattr(basic, 'selinux')\n\n    def test_module_utils_basic_ansible_module_is_special_selinux_path(self):\n        from ansible.module_utils import basic\n\n        args = json.dumps(dict(ANSIBLE_MODULE_ARGS={'_ansible_selinux_special_fs': \"nfs,nfsd,foos\",\n                                                    '_ansible_remote_tmp': \"/tmp\",\n                                                    '_ansible_keep_remote_files': False}))\n\n        with swap_stdin_and_argv(stdin_data=args):\n            basic._ANSIBLE_ARGS = None\n            am = basic.AnsibleModule(\n                argument_spec=dict(),\n            )\n\n            def _mock_find_mount_point(path):\n                if path.startswith('/some/path'):\n                    return '/some/path'\n                elif path.startswith('/weird/random/fstype'):\n                    return '/weird/random/fstype'\n                return '/'\n\n            am.find_mount_point = MagicMock(side_effect=_mock_find_mount_point)\n            am.selinux_context = MagicMock(return_value=['foo_u', 'foo_r', 'foo_t', 's0'])\n\n            m = mock_open()\n            m.side_effect = OSError\n\n            with patch.object(builtins, 'open', m, create=True):\n                self.assertEqual(am.is_special_selinux_path('/some/path/that/should/be/nfs'), (False, None))\n\n            mount_data = [\n                '/dev/disk1 / ext4 rw,seclabel,relatime,data=ordered 0 0\\n',\n                '10.1.1.1:/path/to/nfs /some/path nfs ro 0 0\\n',\n                'whatever /weird/random/fstype foos rw 0 0\\n',\n            ]\n\n            # mock_open has a broken readlines() implementation apparently...\n            # this should work by default but doesn't, so we fix it\n            m = mock_open(read_data=''.join(mount_data))\n            m.return_value.readlines.return_value = mount_data\n\n            with patch.object(builtins, 'open', m, create=True):\n                self.assertEqual(am.is_special_selinux_path('/some/random/path'), (False, None))\n                self.assertEqual(am.is_special_selinux_path('/some/path/that/should/be/nfs'), (True, ['foo_u', 'foo_r', 'foo_t', 's0']))\n                self.assertEqual(am.is_special_selinux_path('/weird/random/fstype/path'), (True, ['foo_u', 'foo_r', 'foo_t', 's0']))\n\n    def test_module_utils_basic_ansible_module_set_context_if_different(self):\n        from ansible.module_utils import basic\n        basic._ANSIBLE_ARGS = None\n\n        am = basic.AnsibleModule(\n            argument_spec=dict(),\n        )\n\n        basic.HAVE_SELINUX = False\n\n        am.selinux_enabled = MagicMock(return_value=False)\n        self.assertEqual(am.set_context_if_different('/path/to/file', ['foo_u', 'foo_r', 'foo_t', 's0'], True), True)\n        self.assertEqual(am.set_context_if_different('/path/to/file', ['foo_u', 'foo_r', 'foo_t', 's0'], False), False)\n\n        basic.HAVE_SELINUX = True\n\n        am.selinux_enabled = MagicMock(return_value=True)\n        am.selinux_context = MagicMock(return_value=['bar_u', 'bar_r', None, None])\n        am.is_special_selinux_path = MagicMock(return_value=(False, None))\n\n        basic.selinux = Mock()\n        with patch.dict('sys.modules', {'selinux': basic.selinux}):\n            with patch('selinux.lsetfilecon', return_value=0) as m:\n                self.assertEqual(am.set_context_if_different('/path/to/file', ['foo_u', 'foo_r', 'foo_t', 's0'], False), True)\n                m.assert_called_with('/path/to/file', 'foo_u:foo_r:foo_t:s0')\n                m.reset_mock()\n                am.check_mode = True\n                self.assertEqual(am.set_context_if_different('/path/to/file', ['foo_u', 'foo_r', 'foo_t', 's0'], False), True)\n                self.assertEqual(m.called, False)\n                am.check_mode = False\n\n            with patch('selinux.lsetfilecon', return_value=1) as m:\n                self.assertRaises(SystemExit, am.set_context_if_different, '/path/to/file', ['foo_u', 'foo_r', 'foo_t', 's0'], True)\n\n            with patch('selinux.lsetfilecon', side_effect=OSError) as m:\n                self.assertRaises(SystemExit, am.set_context_if_different, '/path/to/file', ['foo_u', 'foo_r', 'foo_t', 's0'], True)\n\n            am.is_special_selinux_path = MagicMock(return_value=(True, ['sp_u', 'sp_r', 'sp_t', 's0']))\n\n            with patch('selinux.lsetfilecon', return_value=0) as m:\n                self.assertEqual(am.set_context_if_different('/path/to/file', ['foo_u', 'foo_r', 'foo_t', 's0'], False), True)\n                m.assert_called_with('/path/to/file', 'sp_u:sp_r:sp_t:s0')\n\n        delattr(basic, 'selinux')\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/test/units/module_utils/basic/test_selinux.py",

False\ntry:\n    import selinux\n    HAVE_SELINUX = True\nexcept ImportError:\n    pass\n\n# Python2 & 3 way to get NoneType\nNoneType = type(None)\n\nfrom ansible.module_utils.compat import selectors\n\nfrom ._text import to_native, to_bytes, to_text\nfrom ansible.module_utils.common.text.converters import (\n    jsonify,\n    container_to_bytes as json_dict_unicode_to_bytes,\n true}}'.format(to_native(e)))\n    sys.exit(1)\n\n\nAVAILABLE_HASH_ALGORITHMS = dict()\ntry:\n    import hashlib\n\n    # python 2.7.9+ and 2.7.0+\n    for attribute in ('available_algorithms', 'algorithms'):\n        algorithms = getattr(hashlib, attribute, None)\n        if algorithms:\n            break\n    if algorithms is None:\n        # python 2.5+\n        algorithms = ('md5', 'sha1', 'sha224', 'sha256', 'sha384', 'sha512')\n    for algorithm in algorithms:\n        AVAILABLE_HASH_ALGORITHMS[algorithm] = getattr(hashlib, algorithm)\n\n    # we may have been able to import md5 but it could still not be available\n    try:\n        hashlib.md5()\n    except ValueError:\n        AVAILABLE_HASH_ALGORITHMS.pop('md5', None)\nexcept Exception:\n    import sha\n    AVAILABLE_HASH_ALGORITHMS = {'sha1': sha.sha}\n    try:\n        import md5\n        AVAILABLE_HASH_ALGORITHMS['md5'] = md5.md5\n    except Exception:\n        pass\n\nfrom ansible.module_utils.common._collections_compat import (\n    KeysView,\n    Mapping, MutableMapping,\n    Sequence, MutableSequence,\n    Set, MutableSet,\n)\nfrom ansible.module_utils.common.process import get_bin_path\nfrom ansible.module_utils.common.file import (\n    _PERM_BITS as PERM_BITS,\n    _EXEC_PERM_BITS as EXEC_PERM_BITS,\n    _DEFAULT_PERM as DEFAULT_PERM,\n    is_executable,\n
